commit.message:
perf(kernels): add DequantWorkspace for I2S matmul buffer reuse [CPU] (#3274)

## Summary

Add \DequantWorkspace\ struct and \dequantize_and_matmul_into()\ to
avoid per-call heap allocation of the kn dequantized weight matrix.

### Impact

For a 2B-parameter model FFN layer (k=8192, n=22016), the dequantized
weight buffer is \k*n*4 = ~687 MB\. Previously allocated on every call
to \dequantize_and_matmul()\. Now callers can pre-allocate once and
reuse.

### Changes

- \DequantWorkspace\ with \
ew(k, n)\ and \nsure_capacity(k, n)\
- \dequantize_and_matmul_into()\ workspace-accepting variant
(\#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]\)
- \dequantize_and_matmul()\ now delegates to \_into\ (backward
compatible)
- Export \DequantWorkspace\ from \cpu::mod\

### Tests

3 new tests (all passing):
- \ est_dequant_workspace_matches_allocating\ parity with allocating
variant
- \ est_dequant_workspace_reuse_across_calls\ 3 iterations with same
workspace
- \	est_dequant_workspace_ensure_capacity_grows\  growth without shrink

### Local validation
\\\
cargo clippy -p bitnet-kernels --no-default-features --features cpu --
-D warnings # clean
cargo test -p bitnet-kernels --no-default-features --features cpu --lib
-- quantized_matmul::tests # 138 passed, 0 failed
\\\

**Triage class: A (MERGE NOW)**

commit.message:
Forward stdin through `--connect` to compilation server (#12734)

* Initial plan

* Forward stdin through --connect: client-side thread + server-side pipe forwarding

Add stdin forwarding support for `haxe --connect` so that piped input
(e.g. `echo "data" | haxe --connect port --main Main --interp`) is
forwarded to the compilation server.

Changes:
- compilationContext.ml: Add `stdin` field to `communication` type
- server.ml: Client (`do_connect`) starts a select-based thread that
  reads local stdin and sends to the server socket. Server
  (`init_wait_socket`) creates a pipe + forwarding thread that reads
  stdin data from the client socket and feeds it to the compiler.
  Fixed null-terminator detection in read_loop to handle overflow.
  Added SIGPIPE ignore (signal 13) in wait_loop.
- compiler.ml: Use `comm.stdin` when available instead of empty pipe

Known issue: The server-side forwarding thread blocks on recv(sin)
which can delay connection cleanup for requests that don't use stdin
(e.g. --version). This needs further investigation - potentially using
the socket fd directly via Unix.dup instead of a forwarding thread.

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR feedback: eliminate globals, use Unix.select, extract SocketRequest module, refactor tuples to records

- Point 2: Move current_stdin and current_stdin_pipe from global refs into
  ServerCompilationContext.t. parse_file now takes sctx instead of cs.
  init_wait_socket stores stdin pipe in a local ref returned via get_stdin.

- Point 3: Replace client-side stdin forwarding thread with Unix.select
  loop that multiplexes reading from both server socket and local stdin
  in a single thread.

- Point 4: Extract null-terminated socket reading into SocketRequest module
  with a clean read function that returns {data; overflow}.

- Refactor server_accept from tuple to server_connection record type with
  named fields (support_nonblock, read, write, close, get_stdin).

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* move

* split

* Fix: move process_response() call from before loop to after loop in Connect.poll

The "split" refactoring accidentally moved the final process_response() call
to before the select loop (where it's a no-op on an empty buffer) instead of
keeping it after the loop. This would cause data loss when the server sends
a final response chunk that doesn't end with '\n' before closing the connection.

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix process_response placement in Connect.poll and add connect_stdin integration tests

- Fix: Move process_response() from before the select loop (no-op on empty buffer)
  to after it, restoring the original behavior that flushes any remaining response
  data when the server closes the connection.

- Add tests/misc/connect_stdin/ with three integration tests:
  - stdin line forwarding through --connect
  - stdin multiline forwarding through --connect
  - no-stdin request completes without hanging

- Register connect_stdin tests in CI (Macro target, Linux)

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(core): support tuple validation when schema `items` is an array (JSON Schema draft 07) (#34636)

`validateProperty` in `params.ts` did not handle the case where `items`
is an array — the JSON Schema draft 07 tuple validation form. This
caused false validation failures for schemas like Angular's
`@angular/build:unit-test`, which uses tuple `items` to type reporter
configurations:

```json
{
  "type": "array",
  "minItems": 1,
  "maxItems": 2,
  "items": [
    { "anyOf": [{ "type": "string" }, { "enum": ["junit", "html", ...] }] },
    { "type": "object" }
  ]
}
```

Options like `"reporters": [["junit", {"suiteName": "MyApp"}]]` would
incorrectly fail validation with _"Property 'reporters' does not match
the schema"_.

## Changes

- **`validateProperty`**: when `schema.items` is an array, validates
each element against its positional schema instead of passing the whole
array as a schema
- **`minItems`/`maxItems` enforcement**: array length is now validated
against `minItems` and `maxItems` constraints when present
- **`additionalItems` support**: rejects extra items when
`additionalItems: false`; validates against the `additionalItems` schema
when present; otherwise allows additional items (spec-compliant default)
- **`PropertyDescription` type**: added `minItems`, `maxItems`,
`additionalItems` fields; narrowed `items` from `any` to
`PropertyDescription | PropertyDescription[]`
- **Type guards**: added guards in `coerceType` and
`getPromptsForSchema` where `items.enum` was accessed without accounting
for the array form

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Schema validation fails when `items` is a
list.</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Current Behavior
> 
> In JSON Schema draft 07 (2018), the `"items"` property can be a list
and must be validated using tuple validation.
> 
> The `validateProperty` util in `packages/nx/src/utils/params.ts`
currently does not support this, causing validation to fail with the
error that the property does not match the schema (but it does).
> 
> ### Expected Behavior
> 
> A schema using JSON Schema draft 07 like
https://github.com/angular/angular-cli/blob/15794dc101fffd49818545c4ab015a3bf238b14a/packages/angular/build/src/builders/unit-test/schema.json
must be successfully parsed by the NX parser.
> 
> OR
> 
> The parser must give a clear error message that it does not support
the given schema.
> 
> ### GitHub Repo
> 
> https://github.com/Ionaru/schema7fail
> 
> ### Steps to Reproduce
> 
> In https://github.com/Ionaru/schema7fail:
>

> 2. Run `nx test my-app`

>       "options": {
>         "reporters": [["junit", {"suiteName": "MyApp"}]]
>       }
>     }
> ```
> 3. Run `nx test <app_name>`
>

>

commit.message:
fix: bootstrap airline from TICK_UPDATE when AIRLINE_CREATE missing from relays (#52)

* fix: bootstrap airline from TICK_UPDATE when AIRLINE_CREATE missing from relays

When a competitor's AIRLINE_CREATE event is missing from relay responses
(due to relay pruning, connectivity issues, or relay partitioning),
replayActionLog now pre-scans the action log for a TICK_UPDATE and creates
a synthetic airline entity from its payload. This prevents competitors from
being completely invisible.

Changes:
- actionReducer.ts: Pre-scan for AIRLINE_CREATE; bootstrap from TICK_UPDATE
  with airline identity fields or 'Unknown Airline' placeholders. Added
  'dissolved' flag to ActionReplayResult for proper dissolution cleanup.
- engineSlice.ts: Enrich TICK_UPDATE payload with airline identity metadata
  (name, ICAO, callsign, hubs, livery, status, tier) so bootstrap has
  full data and bankruptcy status propagates live.
- schema.ts: Add retry with exponential backoff (2 retries) to
  publishCheckpoint() to reduce 'Not enough relays' failures.
- worldSlice.ts: Remove dissolved competitors from state instead of
  preserving stale ghost entries. Add warning logs for replay failures.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR feedback for world sync resilience

- reset replay dissolved flag on AIRLINE_CREATE/bootstrap
- use default starting balance for synthetic TICK_UPDATE bootstrap
- retry checkpoint publish only for transient relay/network failures
- include airline identity fields in bankruptcy TICK_UPDATE payload
- add regression tests for bootstrap balance and bankruptcy payload fields

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: avoid replaying pre-bootstrap actions

When replay seeds a synthetic airline from the latest TICK_UPDATE,
older actions were still replayed against snapshot state, which could
double-apply historical deltas and drift reconstructed balances.

This change records bootstrapTick and skips replay for actions with
actionTick <= bootstrapTick. It also captures authoritative fleet/route
IDs directly from the bootstrap TICK_UPDATE payload.

Adds a regression test to verify historical actions are not replayed at
or before the bootstrap tick.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: deploy built assets to gh-pages branch to fix blank page

The blank page was caused by GitHub Pages being configured for
branch-based deployment (serving source files from main), while the
workflow only deployed via the Actions artifact API. The source
index.html references /src/main.tsx which browsers cannot process,
resulting in a blank page.

Root cause: the gh api PUT call to switch build_type to 'workflow'
was silently failing (2>/dev/null || true) because GITHUB_TOKEN lacks
the admin-level permissions needed to change the Pages source config.

Changes:
- Push built dist/ output to a gh-pages branch as a reliable fallback
  for branch-based GitHub Pages deployment
- Remove error suppression on the API call so failures are visible
- Add continue-on-error instead of swallowing errors
- Set enablement: true on configure-pages@v4 so it attempts to enable
  Actions-based deployment
- Change permissions.contents from read to write (needed for git push)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[skills] Add AZDO build investigator skill (#10885)

Add a Copilot skill for investigating Azure DevOps pipeline build
failures. Given a build URL, it fetches run details, identifies failed
jobs/tasks via the timeline API, downloads .binlog artifacts, and
analyzes them with binlogtool to find root causes.

Example usage:

    ❯ what are the errors here: https://github.com/dotnet/android/pull/10881

    ◐ The user is asking about errors in a GitHub PR. This is a CI/build failure investigation task. There's an `azdo-build-investigator` skill available that can investigate Azure DevOps pipeline build failures
    and analyze .binlog files. However, this is a GitHub PR URL, not an AZDO URL directly. Let me check if the skill can handle GitHub PR URLs - the skill description says "Use this when users share an AZDO
    build URL, a GitHub PR URL, or ask about a failing pipeline, build errors, or CI failures." So yes, this skill can handle GitHub PR URLs.

    Let me invoke the `azdo-build-investigator` skill.

    ● skill(azdo-build-investigator)

    ◐ Let me follow the skill workflow to investigate the GitHub PR. The URL is https://github.com/dotnet/android/pull/10881.

    First, let me check if `gh` CLI and `az` CLI are available, and then get the failing checks from the PR.

    ● Get failing checks from PR #10881
    $ gh pr checks 10881 --repo dotnet/android --json "name,state,link,bucket" 2>&1
    └ 39 lines...

    ◐ There's one failing check: `Xamarin.Android-PR` with a link to `https://devdiv.visualstudio.com/0bdbc590-a062-4c3f-b0f6-9383f67865ee/_build/results?buildId=13452282`. Let me parse this:
    - Org: `devdiv` (or `devdiv.visualstudio.com`)
    - Project: `0bdbc590-a062-4c3f-b0f6-9383f67865ee`
    - Build ID: `13452282`

    Let me get the build timeline.

    ● Get failed build timeline records
    $ az devops invoke --area build --resource timeline --route-parameters buildId=13452282 --org https://devdiv.visualstudio.com --project 0bdbc590-a062-4c3f-b0f6-9383f67865ee --query "records[?result=='failed']
    | [].{name:name, type:type, issues:issues, errorCount:errorCount}" --output json 2>&1
    └ 6 lines...

    ● Get failed build timeline records with project as route param
    $ az devops invoke --area build --resource timeline --route-parameters project=0bdbc590-a062-4c3f-b0f6-9383f67865ee buildId=13452282 --org https://devdiv.visualstudio.com --query "records[?result=='failed'] |
    [].{name:name, type:type, issues:issues, errorCount:errorCount}" --output json 2>&1
    └ 182 lines...

    ◐ Now I have a clear picture of the failure. The `Xamarin.Android-PR` check is failing in the `Pack .NET Maui` task with 78 RS0016 errors. These are all Roslyn public API analyzer errors — the new symbols
    added by this PR are not registered in the declared public API files.

    The affected files are in `src\AI\src\Essentials.AI\Platform\MaciOS\`:
    1. `NLEmbeddingExtensions.cs` - `NLEmbeddingExtensions` class and `AsIEmbeddingGenerator` method
    2. `NLEmbeddingGenerator.cs` - `NLEmbeddingGenerator` class, constructor, `GenerateAsync`, `Dispose`

    3. `AppleIntelligenceChatClient.cs` - `AppleIntelligenceChatClient` class, constructor, `GetResponseAsync`, `GetStreamingResponseAsync`

    The root cause is that these new public types and members need to be registered in the `PublicAPI.Shipped.txt` or `PublicAPI.Unshipped.txt` file tracked by the `[MAUIAI0001]` analyzer — this is actually a
    MAUI repo issue where the integration build is failing. The fix requires updating the public API declaration file.

    ● The failing check is Xamarin.Android-PR — specifically the "Pack .NET Maui" task, with 78 RS0016 errors.

    -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    Root Cause: Missing Public API Declarations

    The new Apple Intelligence / MAUI AI types added by this PR are not registered in the public API surface file. All errors are RS0016 from the Roslyn PublicApiAnalyzers:

    "Symbol is not part of the declared public API"

    The affected symbols are all in src\AI\src\Essentials.AI\Platform\MaciOS\ (a MAUI repo path, not this repo):

    ┌──────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    │ File                             │ New Public Symbols                                                  │
    ├──────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    │ AppleIntelligenceChatClient.cs   │ Class, constructor, GetResponseAsync, GetStreamingResponseAsync     │
    ├──────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    │ NLEmbeddingGenerator.cs          │ Class, constructor, GenerateAsync, Dispose                          │
    ├──────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    │ NLEmbeddingExtensions.cs         │ Class, AsIEmbeddingGenerator extension method                       │
    └──────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

    Fix

    The PublicAPI.Unshipped.txt file for the [MAUIAI0001] project needs to be updated to include all these new symbols. This is done in the MAUI repo — you need to add entries like:

    [MAUIAI0001]Microsoft.Maui.Essentials.AI.AppleIntelligenceChatClient
    [MAUIAI0001]Microsoft.Maui.Essentials.AI.AppleIntelligenceChatClient.AppleIntelligenceChatClient() -> void
    [MAUIAI0001]Microsoft.Maui.Essentials.AI.AppleIntelligenceChatClient.GetResponseAsync(...) -> ...
    ...

    to the PublicAPI.Unshipped.txt file in that project. The full list of symbols to add can be found in the 78 error messages from the build log.

environments create on the repo page.

- Workflow YAML from default branch (enforced by issue_comment trigger)
- Validator built from default branch
- Fork content is untrusted data only
- Only write+ users can trigger via permission check

is no longer needed.
